zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Agnes Green是Thomas Green和Susannah Bell的女兒，於1410年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆。她於1431年之前與Robert Crane結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1500年在英國哈普爾享年90歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Green
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: either a nickname for someone who was fond of dressing in this color (Old English grēne) or was young or immature, or who had played the part of the ‘Green Man’ in the May Day celebrations, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a village green (Middle English grene, a transferred use of the color term). This is one of the most common and widespread of English surnames. In North America it has assimilated cognates from other languages, notably German Grün (see Gruen ) and Dutch Groen ; compare 7 below. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: alternatively, from a Middle English personal name Grene.
Irish: adopted for Ó hUainín ‘descendant of Uainín’, a personal name from a pet form of uaine ‘green’, see Honan .

variants.name: Greenman
meanings: English (Wiltshire): from Middle English grene ‘green’ + man ‘man’, a nickname from the Green Man of folklore, or a topographic name for someone who lived by the village green. Compare Green .

Americanized form (translation into English) of German Grunemann or Grünemann: topographic name for someone who lived by the village green (compare above and Greenland ), or a habitational name for someone from any of numerous places called Grüna, Grünau, or Grüne.

variants.name: Greening
meanings: English (Gloucestershire and Dorset): nickname perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English grening, a derivative of grene ‘green’ (Old English grēne), i.e. ‘the green one’, whether physically or metaphorically ‘green’.
English: alternatively, from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Grening, a derivative (originally patronymic) of the Middle English personal name Grene. See Green .
Americanized form of German Grüning, a variant of Groening .

variants.name: Gruen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dieter, Dietrich, Georg, Hans, Heinz, Hermann, Hertha.  Jewish Ehud, Eliezer, Isadore, Isidor, Meyer, Rivka, Shimon, Shlomo, Shoshana.

German (Grün): from Middle High German grūene ‘green, fresh, raw’, hence a nickname for someone who habitually dressed in green, a topographic name for someone who lived in a green and leafy place, or a habitational name for someone from a place called with this word, such as Gruna, Grunau in Silesia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Grün): artificial name from German grün ‘green’. Compare Grun .

variants.name: Greene
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Green  . In North America, this surname has assimilated cognates from other languages.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Agnes Green was born in 1410, in Earl Stonham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Thomas Green and Susannah Bell. She married Robert Crane before 1431. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1500, in Hampshire, England, at the age of 90, and was buried in Chilton, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Agnes Greenは1410年にイギリスイギリススフォルクエアルル ストンハムでThomas GreenとSusannah Bellの娘として生まれた。1431年以前にRobert Craneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1500年にイギリスハンプシュアイアで90歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクチルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Agnes Green(은)는 1410년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 에를 스토함에서 Thomas Green(와)과 Susannah Bell의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1431년 이전 Robert Crane(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1500년 영국 함프실르에서 90세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 치턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Agnes Green родилась в 1410 г. в местности эарл стонам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали Thomas Green и Susannah Bell. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Crane перед 1431 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1500 г. в местности хампшир, Англия в возрасте 90 лет и была похоронена в местности чилтон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
